title: berteroa samolifolia
intro: Berteroa incana, also known as the Hoary Alison or Hoary Whitlowgrass, is a plant species in the rose family, characterized by its grayish or whitish foliage and small white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of compounds like coumarin and alkaloids. The health benefits of Berteroa samolifolia include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and provide antioxidant effects. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from its leaves and flowers, which can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures.
